title: Intersight Device Connector / Tunnel Health
spl:
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:appliance" earliest=-24h
| eval lag_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| where tunnel_state!="connected" OR lag_sec>900
| stats latest(tunnel_state) as tunnel, max(lag_sec) as max_lag_sec by appliance_name
| sort -max_lag_sec
description: If the secure connector or cloud tunnel is down, alarms and inventory stop updating while hardware may still be failing. Early detection preserves single-pane visibility before the operations team loses trust in the portal.
value: If the secure connector or cloud tunnel is down, alarms and inventory stop updating while hardware may still be failing. Early detection preserves single-pane visibility before the operations team loses trust in the portal.
implementation: (1) Ingest appliance health from Intersight add-on or automation hitting the appliance API; (2) alert when tunnel is not connected or sync lag exceeds 15 minutes; (3) correlate with firewall change tickets.
visualization: Table (appliance status), Single value (appliances out of sync), Timechart (sync lag).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Intersight Add-on`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_intersight` `sourcetype="cisco:intersight:appliance"` with fields `appliance_name`, `tunnel_state`, `last_sync_epoch`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest appliance health from Intersight add-on or automation hitting the appliance API; (2) alert when tunnel is not connected or sync lag exceeds 15 minutes; (3) correlate with firewall change tickets.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:appliance" earliest=-24h
| eval lag_sec=now()-last_sync_epoch
| where tunnel_state!="connected" OR lag_sec>900
| stats latest(tunnel_state) as tunnel, max(lag_sec) as max_lag_sec by appliance_name
| sort -max_lag_sec
```

Understanding this SPL

**Intersight Device Connector / Tunnel Health** — If the secure connector or cloud tunnel is down, alarms and inventory stop updating while hardware may still be failing. Early detection preserves single-pane visibility before the operations team loses trust in the portal.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_intersight` `sourcetype="cisco:intersight:appliance"` with fields `appliance_name`, `tunnel_state`, `last_sync_epoch`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Intersight Add-on`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_intersight; **sourcetype**: cisco:intersight:appliance. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_intersight, sourcetype="cisco:intersight:appliance", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lag_sec** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where tunnel_state!="connected" OR lag_sec>900` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by appliance_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (appliance status), Single value (appliances out of sync), Timechart (sync lag).
grandmaExplanation: If the secure connector or cloud tunnel is down, alarms and inventory stop updating while hardware may still be failing — so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
hardware: Cisco Intersight Assist / connected UCS domains
